front: Work: in this play, the white teenager Hally discusses 'a world without collisions' using ballroom dancing as a metaphor, while his servants Sam and Willie practice dancing
Work: in this play, a kite symbolizes the rift between a boy and his servant — the servant couldn't fly it with him because he was on a Whites Only bench
Work: this play ends with the title character spitting in Sam's face after becoming distraught about his father's return from the hospital
Work: in this play, Hally's scalars homework leads to a discussion of Tolstoy, Darwin, and Fleming as 'men of magnitude'
Work: in this play, Sam spends his bus fare to play Sarah Vaughan's 'Little Man' on the jukebox
Work: in this play, brothers Morris (who can pass for white) and Zachariah live in the 'colored' section of Port Elizabeth and exchange letters with white pen-pal Ethel Lange
Work: in this play, two brothers relive a game of 'To Hell and Gone' in a rusted Chevy, imagining driving through a flock of butterflies
Work: in this play, two prisoners rehearse and perform an abridged version of Antigone; originally titled for the guard Hodoshe
back: The Island (Athol Fugard, John Kani, Winston Ntshona)

front: Work: in this play, the title character adopts the identity of the dead Robert Zwelinzima for a work permit and is photographed in Styles's studio
back: Sizwe Bansi Is Dead (Athol Fugard, John Kani, Winston Ntshona)

front: Work: in this play, the elderly widow Miss Helen sculpts a statue garden (including an Easter Island head) based on the real 'Owl House' in New Bethesda
Work: in this play, a couple wanders after their home is bulldozed; an old man (Outa) dies at their campfire; part of the Family Trilogy
Author: this playwright co-founded the Serpent Players in Port Elizabeth with actors John Kani and Winston Ntshona
Author: this playwright inaugurated the Serpent Players with The Cure, an adaptation of Machiavelli's The Mandrake
Work: in this Fugard novel, the title character leads a gang (Aap, Butcher, Boston) and has a turning point when he finds a baby and decides to care for it
Work: this Fugard play features the pacifist teacher Mr. M, killed by revolutionaries after trying to get Isabel and Thami to join a literary contest
Work: this Fugard play about a white man facing guilt after running over a black woman and her baby was called 'my truth and reconciliation' by its author
